title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
summary: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heatJapan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common.The central Japan cit
title_cn: 日本迎来首个“酷热日”，城市酷热达 40 度

title: The First Chatbot’s Multiple Personalities
summary: ELIZA is remembered as the world’s first AI star, a kindly therapist in chatbot form that gently probed users’ worries. Even its creator, Joseph Weizenbaum, was surprised by the warm reception given to his experiment in human-machine interaction. For some, it heralded an age of automated psychotherapy, while others believed the program demonstrated sentience, a fallacy soon known as the “ELIZA effect.” Based on published descriptions, ELIZA has been implemented on many different computers, but o
title_cn: 第一个聊天机器人的多重性格
summary_cn: ELIZA 被认为是世界上第一位人工智能明星，是一位以聊天机器人形式出现的友善治疗师，可以温和地探究用户的担忧。就连它的创造者 Joseph Weizenbaum 也对它受到的热烈欢迎感到惊讶。

title: Why AI Needs a “Genie Coefficient”
summary: Major benchmarks measure what AI can do. None measure whether it does what you mean: the distance between what you ask an AI to do and the unspoken assumptions about how you want the AI to do it. We propose a new metric: the Genie coefficient.There’s often a gap between one person’s request and another’s understanding. Most of the time, we bridge it using general knowledge. title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortuna

title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agen
